viewing-file-contents.label: Viewing File Contents
viewing-file-contents.summary: Use `cat`, `head`, `tail`, and `wc -l` to inspect text files quickly.

sketch0.title: cat prints a whole text file

sketch1.title: head and tail preview file parts

sketch2.title: wc counts lines, words, and bytes

ci_create_notes_summary.title: Preview a report and a log

try_viewing_file_contents_sketch1.title: Preview a report and a log
try_viewing_file_contents_sketch1.prompt: Run `head reports/week.txt` and `tail logs/server.log`, then create `review/previewed.txt`.

ci-create-notes-summary.hint: `head` reads from the top; `tail` reads from the bottom.

cat-shows-whole-file.title: `cat` shows a whole file
cat-shows-whole-file.bodyMarkdown: `cat file.txt` prints the full file. It is best for short notes, small config files, and quick checks.

head-tail-preview.title: `head` and `tail` show the edges
head-tail-preview.bodyMarkdown: `head` previews the beginning of a file. `tail` checks the end. They are useful when a file is too long to read all at once.

wc-counts.title: `wc -l` counts lines
wc-counts.bodyMarkdown: `wc -l file.txt` prints the number of lines. That is handy for logs, lists, and data files.
